EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words): With type 1 diabetes, excessive thirst, frequent urination, and nausea are classic warning signs that your blood sugar may be running very high, which can lead to a dangerous condition called diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA). DKA can escalate from mild symptoms to a life-threatening emergency within hours. The fact that you're not confused and can keep fluids down is reassuring right now, but this situation can worsen quickly and unpredictably. Ideally, you should check your blood sugar and test for ketones (urine strips or blood ketone meter) immediately. If your blood sugar is very high (above 300 mg/dL) or ketones are moderate to large, or if you develop vomiting, rapid breathing, confusion, or worsening symptoms, you need emergency care regardless of cost — DKA can be fatal without treatment. I understand the financial concern, but delaying care if this is DKA could be far more dangerous and costly.
